topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The blue light of the screen was the only illumination in Maya’s cramped dorm room. She scrolled mindlessly through feeds, the curated perfection of others a sharp contrast to the quiet emptiness surrounding her. A notification popped up, a familiar profile picture—Mr. Harrison. Her breath hitched. She clicked, expecting a syllabus update, but instead found himself in the stream of his recent posts: a picture of a hiking trail, a casual remark about a new book. A slow, heavy feeling settled in her chest, a peculiar tightening that made her want to curl into herself. He wasn't just a figure in a lecture hall anymore; he was actively observing her digital existence. Maya stared at the screen, her fingers hovering over the 'unfollow' button, a strange mixture of alarm and morbid fascination swirling within her.

[story 2]

Liam traced the condensation on his water bottle, the rhythmic dripping the only sound cutting through the silence of his apartment. The evening had stretched into an endless, muted gray. He was supposed to be studying, but his gaze kept drifting to his phone, a compulsive habit he couldn't seem to break. Then, a notification flashed: a direct message from Professor Evans. It was a simple, almost mundane greeting, referencing a shared class assignment. Liam felt a sudden, unwelcome jolt, a strange awareness that someone, someone he only saw in the context of academic obligation, was tracking his activities. He quickly minimized the chat, his jaw clenching. The quiet in the room suddenly felt oppressive, magnified by this unexpected intrusion into his private space.

[story 3]

Sarah sat by the window, watching the city lights blur into streaks of neon. The silence of the late hour pressed in on her, heavy and suffocating. She was re-reading an old essay, the words blurring before her eyes, seeking any anchor in the familiar structure of academia. Her phone buzzed. It was a photo—a candid shot from a recent campus event, clearly taken by someone close to the action. It was Mr. Davies. A flicker of something unsettled her; he wasn't supposed to be documenting these things. A cold prickle ran down her spine. She didn't look away immediately, instead letting her gaze linger on the image, a strange, involuntary stillness taking over her posture.
